name: Bougatsa
recipeInstructions:
1. Using an electric mixer, beat the cream cheese at high speed until light and fluffy.
2. Lower the speed and add the ricotta, cottage cheese, 1 1/2 tablespoons sugar, and nutmeg. Beat for 1 minute at high speed, then set aside while you prepare the filo.
3. Lay the filo flat on a table and keep covered with a damp towel over waxed paper or plastic wrap. Stack 4 sheets, brushing the top of each sheet with melted butter as you stack them to form a base of 15 x 18 inches.
4. Lay 2 sheets at right angles over the center, forming a diamond shape, brushing the center of each with butter.
5. Divide the filling into 3 parts and spread one part over the filo to form a 7-inch square. Set the rest of the filling aside.
6. Fold the top filo sheet over the cheese and brush with butter, continuing to fold the filo over the cheese, brushing each time with butter until all edges are covered.
7. With a wide spatula, lift the bougatsa and invert onto a cookie sheet, brushing the top with butter.
8. Repeat with the remaining filo, filling until all 3 square pies are folded.
9. Bake in a 350 degree F (175 C) oven for 15 to 20 minutes or until the filo puffs up and turns a golden chestnut color.
10. Cut into small squares and sprinkle with additional sugar.
11. Serve piping hot.
